questions.question: List the six fields of the Bitcoin block header and state the size (in bytes) of each.
Explain the concept of a Merkle Tree. Describe how it is constructed from a set of transactions and explain its two main advantages for the Bitcoin protocol.
expectedPoints: Definition: A binary tree of cryptographic hashes.
Construction: Start with transaction hashes. Hash pairs together repeatedly until only one hash remains (the Merkle Root).

Advantage 1: Efficiency: Allows for Simplified Payment Verification (SPV) where a client can verify a transaction's inclusion using only a block header and a Merkle path (log2(N) hashes), without needing the full block.
Advantage 2: Integrity: Any change to any transaction will propagate up the tree and cause a completely different Merkle Root, invalidating the block's header and proof-of-work.
